Context: This foreword introduces the JLL and NAREDCO Maharashtra report, "Unlocking Opportunities with Infrastructure Development - A Comprehensive Analysis of Mumbai & Pune," published in September 2023. It highlights Maharashtra's ongoing infrastructure transformation, including projects like the Mumbai Nagpur Expressway and Navi Mumbai International Airport, and their profound impact on the state's real estate sector.

Chunk Content: Unlocking Opportunities with Infrastructure Development

# Foreword

I am delighted to introduce our report titled “Unlocking Opportunities through Infrastructure Development - A Comprehensive Analysis of Mumbai & Pune.” This study, a collaborative effort between NAREDCO Maharashtra and JLL, serves as a testament to the transformative journey currently underway in the state of Maharashtra.

Maharashtra, often hailed as the economic powerhouse of our nation, is in the midst of a remarkable transformation driven by the visionary approach of its government towards infrastructure development. The unwavering commitment to strategic investments in infrastructure is not only propelling the state to new heights but also redefining the very essence of urban existence. Some of the flagship projects at the forefront of this monumental shift include the Hindu Hrudaysamrat Balasaheb Thackeray Maharashtra Samruddhi Mahamarg (Mumbai Nagpur Expressway), the Mumbai Ahmedabad Bullet Train, the Navi Mumbai International Airport, the Mumbai Coastal Roads, the Mumbai Trans Harbour Sea Link Project, the Mumbai Goa Konkan Expressway, the Santacruz-Chembur Link Road, the Pune Ring Road, a network of Metro Lines across various cities, and more.

The convergence of visionary leadership and the meticulous execution of these ambitious projects heralds a profound impact on the real estate sector.

As we delve into the contents of this report, we will unveil how Maharashtra’s substantial investments in infrastructure are steering a transformative shift that reverberates across the state. We will uncover emerging micro markets and hotspots that have been created through enhanced accessibility and connectivity, allowing peripheral locations to seamlessly integrate with the city. This integration paves the way for new opportunities in real estate development and investment.

Furthermore, we will also reveal existing micro markets benefitting from these infrastructure enhancements, rendering them highly attractive for real estate ventures and investments. Infrastructure upgrades are breathing new life into established markets, as improved amenities, public spaces, and accessibility rejuvenate these areas, making them more appealing to residents, businesses, and investors alike.

In conclusion, let this thought-provoking piece not only stimulate reflection but also inspire decisive action. The opportunities that lie before us are boundless, and it is our shared responsibility to harness them, for the betterment of Maharashtra and the advancement of the real estate sector.

**Sandeep Runwal**
President, NAREDCO Maharashtra
Managing Director, Runwal Developers

Context: This preface introduces a JLL – NAREDCO Maharashtra report published in September 2023, focusing on infrastructure development in Maharashtra and its economic benefits. The report specifically analyzes the impact of these developments on the real estate sector in Mumbai and Pune.

Chunk Content: Unlocking Opportunities with Infrastructure Development

# Preface

India is currently the fifth largest economy in the world and is slated to be among the fastest growing over the coming years. A significant precursor to these development goals being realized is infrastructure capacity augmentation. The fundamental role of infrastructure in contributing to a nation’s sustainable growth in the long run cannot be overemphasized. Taking cognizance of this fact, the Government of India gave birth to the National Infrastructure Pipeline, with a projected investment of ~INR 111 lac crore over FY 2020–25. The objective was to develop a comprehensive view of infrastructure development, monitor progress to ensure timely execution and boost investments in the sector. Additionally, the PM Gati Shakti project was introduced in the latest budget to bring in further convergence between the centre and states for integrated planning of infrastructure projects. This has resulted in an unprecedented expansion of roads, railways and waterways, whereas ports and airports have been substantially upgraded.

In keeping with the winds of change around the country, Maharashtra has also taken remarkable strides towards rapid economic development through its extensive infrastructure development plan. It has strategically focused on constructing major roads, highways and airports to connect major economic centers with emerging hubs of economic activity. By investing heavily in multi-modal connectivity projects, the state has created a network of efficient corridors that have the ability to facilitate the movement of goods, people, and ideas at rapid pace to support development goals.

The impact of these infrastructure initiatives extends significantly to the real estate sector, benefiting all major asset classes. In particular, increased economic activity, driven by infrastructure investments, has led to a growing demand for housing. As prosperity rises and populations migrate to both existing urban centers and emerging cities, the need for quality residential spaces becomes even more evident.

To address this significant social need, the government of Maharashtra has provided crucial support through policy interventions and fiscal incentives. These measures have not only spurred the growth of the housing sector but have also contributed to the vitality visible across major cities in the state. Against this backdrop, this report explores the major infrastructure projects being implemented in Maharashtra and analyzes their economic benefits by focusing on its two biggest economic centres – Mumbai and Pune.

By examining the interplay between infrastructure development and the real estate sector, the report seeks to provide valuable insights into the existing opportunities and potential growth areas with specific focus on the residential sector’s performance in the two cities. These two major cities serve as barometers for assessing the demand and overall activity levels in other cities within the state. Through a comprehensive analysis of above mentioned factors, this report hopes to offer valuable insights to investors, developers, and stakeholders interested in the diverse opportunities within Maharashtra’s flourishing real estate market.

**Karan Singh Sodi**
Senior Managing Director, Mumbai MMR & Gujarat
Head - Alternatives, India

## Fueling India’s growth engine

| No. | Key Indicator | Details |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| 1 | **Highest contribution to India’s GDP** | Maharashtra’s average contribution to India’s nominal GDP from 2011-12 to 2021-22 stands at **14.0%** |

| 3 | **Industrial powerhouse** | Maharashtra’s contribution to the industrial output in India is **15%** |
| 4 | **India’s financial and manufacturing hub** | State’s capital, Mumbai, is the commercial capital of India and has evolved into a global financial hub. Pune, another major city in the state, has emerged as a strong technology & manufacturing hub of India |
| 5 | **World-class infrastructure** | Home to 5 international & 13 domestic airports, 2 major and 48 non-major ports; Maharashtra has the **highest share in total installed capacity of electricity generation in the country** |
| 6 | **Biggest contributor to India’s residential real estate market** | Mumbai and Pune together account for **almost 50% of the total residential sales value in India** |

# Infrastructure steers real estate growth
## Impact on real estate cannot be painted using the same brush

Infrastructure upgrades hold a profound influence over the real estate market, shaping development patterns, spurring market evolution while impacting property values, demand and overall quality of life. However, it’s crucial to acknowledge that the impact of infrastructure development on real estate growth cannot be painted using the same brush. A multitude of factors, including the scale of the project, location, local market conditions, and the economic climate, contribute to the variability in impact.

*   **Immediate impact on property values:** Typically, following the project announcement and nearing completion, property values tend to appreciate. However, during the construction phase, property values have the potential to rise or even decrease, depending on the inconveniences faced within the impact zones and delays in completion.
*   **Emergence of newer markets and hot spots:** By enhancing accessibility and connectivity, peripheral locations integrate more seamlessly with the city, paving the way for new opportunities in real estate development and investment.
*   **Improved access to business districts:** Leads to reduced commute times and heightened convenience for professionals. This, in turn, generates increased demand for real estate in proximity to these bustling business hubs.
*   **Congestion relief and reduced commute time:** Alleviate strain on existing transportation systems, resulting in shorter travel times and enhanced overall efficiency. As a result, areas benefitting from these improvements become highly attractive for real estate ventures and investments.
*   **Revitalization of established markets:** Infrastructure upgrades bring about a facelift to old, established markets. By improving amenities, public spaces, and accessibility, these areas are rejuvenated and become more appealing to residents, businesses, and investors alike.

In conclusion, infrastructure upgrades stand as a vital catalyst in driving real estate value, demand, and overall growth. By acknowledging the distinct opportunities presented by these enhancements, investors, developers, and policymakers gain the ability to make informed choices, thereby optimizing the potential of the undertaken initiatives.

**The subsequent section delves deeper into the impact of selected infrastructure projects in the well-established city of Mumbai.**

# Santacruz-Chembur Link Road
## Major facelift of established real estate markets

The Santacruz–Chembur Link Road (SCLR) is a 6.45-km-long arterial road in Mumbai, connecting the Western Express Highway (WEH) with the Eastern Express Highway (EEH).

### Project Timeline
*   **2002:** SCLR announced
*   **2007:** Construction commencement
*   **2014:** Opened to the public

### Impact on Residential Capital Values

| Location | Increase in Residential Capital Values (2002-2007) | Increase in Residential Capital Values (2007-2014) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Chembur | 83% | 134% |
| Ghatkopar | 79% | 93% |
| Mumbai | 72% | 61% |

### Chembur underwent a metamorphosis
*   Major facelift post implementation of Eastern Freeway, SCLR, Metro rail and Monorail
*   Massive redevelopment and slum rehabilitation projects undertaken by developers
*   Nearly **8,000 units launched between 2014 to 2019**, higher than the previous 10 years
*   Units sold more than tripled in 2014-19 as compared to 2008-13

Chunk Content: Unlocking Opportunities with Infrastructure Development

# Versova-Andheri-Ghatkopar Metro Line
## Growth triggered in already established markets

The Versova-Andheri-Ghatkopar (VAG) Line or Mumbai Metro 1 Blue Line is a part of the metro system for the city of Mumbai and connects Versova in the Western Suburbs to Ghatkopar in the Eastern Suburbs.

### Project Timeline
*   **2004:** Metro Phase I announced
*   **2008:** Construction commencement
*   **2014:** Operations commenced

### Impact on Residential Capital Values

| Location | Increase in Residential Capital Values (2004-2008) | Increase in Residential Capital Values (2008-2014) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Andheri | 147% | 88% |
| Ghatkopar | 58% | 64% |
| Mumbai | 53% | 59% |

### Andheri revamped as a prominent office and residential market
*   Major boost to Sakinaka, Marol and Chakala office markets with growth of marquee buildings and changing occupier mix
*   Nearly **15,000 units launched post 2008** by prominent developers such as Runwal, Lodha, Hubtown, Kanakia
*   Units sold increased by **~50% in 2014-19** as compared to 2008-13

### Ghatkopar back on real estate landscape
*   Significant gains owing to SCLR, eastern freeway and Metro rail
*   Large Developers such as Runwal, Wadhwa and Kalpataru launched projects
*   Units sold more than doubled in 2014-19 as compared to 2008-13

Context: This JLL – NAREDCO Maharashtra report, published in September 2023, analyzes the impact of infrastructure development on real estate in Mumbai and Pune. This specific section details the historical reliance on Mumbai's suburban rail network, its current challenges, and the modal share of different transport modes in 2016, highlighting the need for planned metro development.

Chunk Content: # Suburban rail is the lifeline of Mumbai with a modal share of 43%

* The robust Mumbai suburban rail network has transported people for more than 150 years. However, the rapid expansion of Mumbai has resulted in more and more people living away from central Mumbai
* This has led to a mismatch between the location of jobs and the location of the working population. People need to cover long distances to commute to work on a daily basis. For such people, the Mumbai suburban rail network has been the primary mode of commuting
* Consequently, the Mumbai suburban rail network experiences ‘super dense crush load’ conditions with a density of 14 passengers per square meter during periods of peak demand. Even buses face over-capacity utilization loads
* Due to the reasons mentioned above, the overall share of public transport has gradually decreased over the last few decades. It has dropped from 88% in 1991 to 65% in 2016
* The planned development of the Mumbai metro corridors will halt the decline in the share of public transport and offer easier accessibility, safer transit, and comfortable journeys to a significant number of people

| Transport Mode | Modal Share (%) |
| :--- | :---: |
| Rail | 43% |
| Bus | 20% |
| Taxi/Rickshaw | 14% |
| Two-wheeler | 12% |
| Car | 9% |
| Metro | 2% |
| **Total Motorized (Taxi, Car, Two-wheeler)** | **35%** |

**Caption:** Modal share of transport: 2016. Source: Comprehensive Mobility Plan for Greater Mumbai, 2016

Context: The report, published by JLL in September 2023, analyzes infrastructure development in Mumbai and Pune. This section specifically discusses how upcoming infrastructure projects in Mumbai are expected to impact public transport modal share and residential property values by 2030.

Chunk Content: # Bringing the suburbs and peripheral markets towards price parity

The realization of the infrastructure development plan can herald a new era for public transportation in Mumbai. It is expected to drive a modal shift away from motorized vehicles, while the share of metro transport and monorail is likely to increase significantly. Resultantly, the modal share of public transport is expected to increase from 65% in 2016 to 75% in 2030. In addition to an increase in public transport modal share, the metro network will improve the convenience as well as safety of commuting.

Given the high traffic flow and rush to employment centers during peak hours, staying close to office markets has been of great importance. Consequently, residential clusters in proximity to office markets have flourished and attract steep prices whereas those with limited connectivity have trailed. Similar to the first metro corridor, i.e. VAG, the upcoming corridors will benefit residential pockets in their influence zones and ultimately lead to a reduction in the residential price gradient witnessed across submarkets in Mumbai.

| Transport Mode | 2016 Share | 2030F Share |
| :--- | :---: | :---: |
| Rail | 43% | 30% |
| Motorized | 35% | 25% |
| Bus | 20% | 10% |
| Metro | 2% | 35% |

**Caption:** Towards a multimodal public transit system. Source: Comprehensive Mobility Plan for Greater Mumbai, 2016; JLL Research

# Government measures to support the real estate sector
### Proactive measures to ensure sustainable growth

The Maharashtra Government has been proactive in implementing timely measures to support the housing sector. The section below looks at select important measures which have supported the post COVID revival of the housing sector.

#### Short term measures

**Stamp Duty Reduction:**
Maharashtra was the first state to reduce stamp duty post Covid (300 basis points until Dec 31, 2020, and 200 basis points until March 31, 2021). This initiative played a huge role in boost residential sales, both on the primary and secondary market.

**Reduction in Construction Premium:**
In January 2021, the Maharashtra Government slashed premiums charged on construction by 50 per cent for the next one year. The developers, who took the benefit of the 50 per cent waiver in premium had to pay the entire stamp duty amount on behalf of customers.

#### Structural measures

**Unified Development Control and Promotion Regulations:**
The Unified DCPR offers increased FSI, through the introduction of ‘ancillary FSI’, flexibility in FSI utilization and clarity in regulations related to it while incentivizing sustainable housing. These benefits will contribute to a more streamlined construction activity in the housing sector in Maharashtra, promoting growth, affordability, and sustainable development.

**Fast track self–redevelopment projects:**
In order to make self redevelopment more viable, the State Government has reduced stamp duty on self-redevelopment project allotments to INR 1,000, instead of the current rate which varies between 5% - 7% of the total agreement value. Additionally, a dedicated single window is being created to ensure approvals for self–redevelopment projects within 3 months. The process of obtaining bank loans will also be simplified.

**Maharashtra Real Estate Regulatory Authority (MahaRERA):**
RERA was introduced in order to promote transparency, accountability, and efficiency in the real estate industry and Maharashtra was the first state to implement RERA. The State is way ahead in terms of implementation of the Act and it sets a benchmark for other states to emulate.

Chunk Content: Unlocking Opportunities with Infrastructure Development

# The MahaRERA Impact
### Increased transparency within the real estate sector

Maharashtra was the first state to take an initiative to adapt to a regulatory authority for the housing sector with Maharashtra Legislative assembly having cleared the Housing (Regulation and Development) Bill in 2012. The State subsequently adopted the RERA regulation in 2017 by establishing the Maharashtra Real Estate Regulatory Authority (referred to as MahaRERA) and appointing the Authority Chairperson and Members in May 2017. Some of the initiatives of the MahaRERA that have increased transparency within the real estate sector in the state are outlined here.

**Conciliation forum:**
MahaRERA formed the Conciliation forum in March 2018 to enable the disputed parties, both homebuyers and developers to connect and appear before the expert conciliators to resolve their problems, clear doubts, and mitigate misunderstandings. The forum received 1095 conciliation cases of which 554 cases have been disposed of

**Digital mapping:**
MahaRERA has digitally mapped the entire state to help homebuyers get information on their projects and social and physical infrastructure of an area where projects are coming up

**Virtual hearings and helpline service:**
During the pandemic lockdown, MahaRERA used online modes like video conferencing to restart regular hearing of cases to avoid delays. An online helpdesk was set up where homebuyers could call to resolve their queries

This specific section details the market dynamics of Mumbai's residential sector, including sales, launches, and affordability trends from 2018 to a 2023 forecast.

Chunk Content: Unlocking Opportunities with Infrastructure Development

# Mumbai: Market Dynamics
### Value of residential sales expected to cross INR 1 lakh crore in 2023

**Sales of residential units touch new heights**
(Caption: Residential unit sales in INR cr.)

| Year | Period | Sales (INR cr.) |
| :--- | :--- | :---: |
| 2018 | Pre - COVID | 66,820 |
| 2019 | Pre - COVID | 60,928 |
| 2022 | Post - COVID | 90,552 |
| H1 2023 | Post - COVID | 50,075 |

**Increased focus on the upper mid and premium segments**
(Caption: Sales & new launches in upper mid & premium segments in units)

| Period | New launches | Sales |
| :--- | :---: | :---: |
| Pre - COVID | 17,500 | 11,000 |
| Post - COVID | 20,000 | 14,000 |

**Affordability expected to stay below the threshold level**
(Caption: Home Purchase Affordability Index)

| Year | Index Score |
| :--- | :---: |
| 2018 | [unlabeled] |
| 2019 | 90 |
| 2020 | 94 |
| 2021 | 100 |
| 2022 | 92 |
| 2023E | 92 |

**Key Trends**
* Mumbai accounts for ~40% of the total residential sales value in India
* In terms of number of units, the market accounts for ~25% of the total sales volume across the top seven cities
* The market has witnessed a strong recovery in sales with 2022 surpassing 2018, both in terms of sales value and sales volume
* In 2023, sales of residential units in Mumbai is expected to increase further – **residential sales value is likely to breach the INR 1 lac crore mark**
* Post COVID, launch of residential units also witnessed an uptick, increasing by ~183% y-o-y in 2022.
* H1 2023 launches have already crossed 50% of the total launches in 2022
* Mumbai has been the fastest-moving city in terms of its Home Purchase Affordability index score movement and became an affordable market with its threshold hitting 100 in 2021
* Affordability was highest in 2021 amid decadal low interest rates, attractive prices and household income recovery
* It dropped below the threshold in 2022 and is likely to remain so in the short term but still see its affordability levels remain second best in the last five years
* While mid segment projects still account for most of the market activity in Mumbai, the focus on upper mid and premium segments has increased Post-COVID
* In the 1.5 years (Jan 2022 - June 2023), launches and sales within the above mentioned segments have already surpassed levels witnessed over 2 years (Jan 2018 – Dec 2019)

**Note:**
1. Upper mid segment: INR 2 crore – INR 3 crore; Premium segment: INR 3 crore – INR 5 crore
2. Pre-COVID time period: Jan 2018 – Dec 2019; Post-COVID time period: Jan 2022 – June 2023

**Source:** JLL Research

This specific chunk details the residential market dynamics in Pune, including sales, new launches, and affordability trends from 2018 to H1 2023, with projections for the full year 2023.

Chunk Content: Unlocking Opportunities with Infrastructure Development

# Pune: Market Dynamics
### Value of residential sales expected to cross INR 40,000 crore in 2023

**Residential sales witness a threefold increase in value terms**
(Caption: Residential unit sales in INR cr.)

| Year | Period | Sales (INR cr.) |
| :--- | :--- | :---: |
| 2018 | Pre - COVID | 9,287 |
| 2019 | Pre - COVID | 9,913 |
| 2022 | Post - COVID | 25,525 |
| H1 2023 | Post - COVID | 20,929 |

**Value shift towards the mid and upper mid segments**
(Caption: Sales & new launches in mid & upper mid segments in units)

| Period | New launches | Sales |
| :--- | :---: | :---: |
| Pre - COVID | 12,284 | 10,680 |
| Post - COVID | 66,590 | 44,190 |

**One of the most affordable cities in India**
(Caption: Home Purchase Affordability Index)

| Year | Index Score |
| :--- | :---: |
| 2018 | 167 |
| 2019 | 178 |
| 2020 | 186 |
| 2021 | 195 |
| 2022 | 183 |
| 2023E | 183 |

**Key Trends**
* Pune accounts for ~11% of the total residential sales value in India
* The market has witnessed a strong recovery in sales with 2022 surpassing 2018, both in terms of sales value and sales volume
* In 2023, sales of residential units in Pune is expected to increase further – **residential sales value is likely to cross INR 40,000 crore**
* Post COVID, launch of residential units also witnessed an uptick, increasing by ~110% y-o-y in 2022.
* H1 2023 launches have already touched 70% of the total launches in 2022
* Pune to remain one of the most affordable cities, however upward price movement, higher loan interest rates to have an impact on 2023 affordability levels
* Mid and upper mid segment projects still account for most of the market activity in Pune
* In the 1.5 years (Jan 2022 - June 2023), launches and sales within the above mentioned segments have already surpassed levels witnessed over 2 years (Jan 2018 – Dec 2019)

**Note:**
1. Mid segment: INR 0.5 crore – INR 1 crore; Upper mid segment: INR 1 crore – INR 2 crore
2. Pre-COVID time period: Jan 2018 – Dec 2019; Post-COVID time period: Jan 2022 – June 2023
3. Pre-COVID, launches in the mid & upper mid segments accounted for 35% of the total new launches. Post, COVID, this increased to 80%
4. Pre-COVID, sales in the mid & upper mid segments accounted for 26% of the total new sales. Post, COVID, this increased to 73%

**Source:** JLL Research

Chunk Content: Unlocking Opportunities with Infrastructure Development

# Vision 2030

### India to surpass INR 6 lac crore in residential sales value

| Metric | Details |
| :--- | :--- |
| **350 acres of land acquired for residential** | In the last 18 months, developers have acquired a total of ~350 acres of land in Mumbai and Pune, for the purpose of residential development. |
| **Development potential of >50 mn sq ft** | Developers will monetize these land banks in the future. The resultant residential development potential is ~53 mn sq ft. |
| **Sale potential of INR 50,000 cr** | The monetization of these land banks in itself, results in a sales potential of close to INR 50,000 crore. |
| **Healthy momentum in sales and capital value appreciation is expected to continue** | Residential prices are expected to increase at a CAGR of 6%-7% over the next 5-7 years. Sales of residential units will also continue on an upward trajectory. |
| **2030: Residential sales in the top seven cities of India** | Expected to grow over 2X to exceed INR 6 lac crore in value terms. |
| **Mumbai Market Outlook** | In residential sales value terms, the size of the Mumbai market is estimated to double and cross the INR 2 lakh crore mark. |
| **Pune Market Outlook** | With the Pune residential market growing at a rapid pace, Pune’s contribution to the total value of residential sales is expected to increase over the next 5-7 years to ~INR 1 lac crore. |

**Caption:** Vision 2030 projections for residential sales value and development potential in India, specifically focusing on Mumbai and Pune.

For over 200 years, JLL (NYSE: JLL), a leading global commercial real estate and investment management company, has helped clients buy, build, occupy, manage and invest in a variety of commercial, industrial, hotel, residential and retail properties. A Fortune 500® company with annual revenue of $20.9 billion and operations in over 80 countries around the world, our more than 103,000 employees bring the power of a global platform combined with local expertise. ### About JLL India
JLL is India’s premier and largest professional services firm specialising in real estate. The Firm has grown from strength to strength in India for the past two decades. JLL India has an extensive presence across 10 major cities (Mumbai, Delhi NCR, Bengaluru, Pune, Chennai, Hyderabad, Kolkata, Ahmedabad, Kochi and Coimbatore) and over 130 tier-II and III markets with a cumulative strength is over 13,000 professionals. The Firm provides investors, developers, local corporates and multinational companies with a comprehensive range of services. These include leasing, capital markets, research & advisory, transaction management, project development, facility management and property & asset management. These services cover various asset classes such as commercial, industrial, warehouse and logistics, data centres, residential, retail, hospitality, healthcare, senior living, and education. For further information, please visit jll.co.in

### About JLL Research
JLL Research provides data analytics and insights through Real Estate Intelligence Services (REIS), thought leadership and bespoke research. REIS is a subscription based research service designed to provide cutting edge insights into diverse and challenging real estate markets through collation, analysis and forecasts of property market indicators across asset classes such as office, retail and residential. Thought leadership focuses on providing independent insights, analysis and forecasts on key industry trends and significant regulatory & economic developments impacting the real estate industry. Bespoke research aims to provide tailor-made solutions to different stakeholders in the real estate sector and ancillary industries. Our capabilities include market assessment studies, demand-supply analysis, catchment area analysis, and price benchmarking across asset classes.

### About NAREDCO
National Real Estate Development Council (“NAREDCO”) is established as an autonomous self-regulatory body in 1998 under the aegis of Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs, Govt. of India. Since its inception in 1998, NAREDCO has relentlessly forged meaningful collaborations with both the public and private sectors, positioning itself as a thriving platform for collective growth. Over the years, it has grown into a nationwide movement, bringing together more than 10,000 respected developers who excel in their respective fields. Through its extensive network of State Chapters, NAREDCO has cultivated grassroots presence, ensuring that its influence extends to every corner of the country. Together, NAREDCO has fostered an environment that encourages collaboration, innovation, and transformative advancement.

NAREDCO has established State Chapters across the country, thereby ensuring that the Policy Recommendations by NAREDCO are a true reflection of ground realities. NAREDCO Maharashtra has further established various Region / City Chapters deepening the roots in the state.

Over the past few years, NAREDCO Maharashtra has emerged as a catalyst for industry growth in the state and has introduced several innovative platforms including “The Real Estate Forum” – Annual Mega-conference on Real Estate in the State, “Homethon Expo” - Grand scale three - day exhibition, “Realty of Budget” - Annual Event to decode key announcements and benefits under India’s Budget for Real Estate Sector, “Mentorship sessions” – Close Door exclusive mentorship sessions with Doyen of Real Estate Industry, “MahaRERA Helpdesk” at MahaRERA office for handholding members in Project Registration and Compliance, “NAREDCO Urbanopedia” – Online Knowledge Sessions on various issues of sector and many more.
